hypothesis: Iterations 24-27 have consistently demonstrated genuine OOS alpha (OOS Sharpe > IS Sharpe) with the 5/15 SMA crossover per-ETF architecture. The three remaining failures are: (1) IS Sharpe ~0.54 < 1.0, (2) IS MaxDD ~-22% > -20%, (3) IS trades ~37-45 < 50. The RSI<60 filter in iter 27 reduced trades further without helping IS Sharpe. The core fix this iteration: (1) REMOVE the RSI filter entirely — return to pure SMA crossover entries, (2) Tighten trailing stop to 6% (from 8%) to finally push IS MaxDD below -20%, and (3) Lower the slow SMA from 15 to 12 days to generate more crossovers per year per ETF. A 5/12 SMA crossover fires ~20+ times per year per ETF vs ~15 for 5/15, targeting 8 ETFs × 20 × 5 years = 800 potential events, well above 50. The tighter 6% trailing stop cuts losers faster, which both reduces MaxDD AND improves IS Sharpe by avoiding large losing trades. No regime filter, no absolute momentum filter, no cross-sectional ranking — pure independent per-ETF SMA crossover. Long-only avoids short-side losses.

evaluation.post_mortem: This iteration (v4) achieved a rare and genuinely encouraging signal: OOS Sharpe (0.667) substantially exceeds IS Sharpe (0.353), and the walk-forward consistency score is a perfect 1.0 with 4/7 windows passing. This strongly suggests real OOS alpha with minimal overfitting risk. However, the strategy fails on three hard acceptance criteria: (1) IS Sharpe of 0.353 (post-cost: 0.266) is far below the 1.0 threshold — the 5/12 SMA crossover generates too many noisy trades with low average profitability; (2) IS Max Drawdown of -26.6% breaches the -20% limit — the 6% trailing stop did not sufficiently curtail drawdowns, likely because the faster 5/12 crossover creates more whipsaw entries that compound losses before stops trigger; (3) OOS Sharpe of 0.667 (post-cost: 0.539) also falls short of the 0.70 target. On the positive side: trade count (54.25 IS) finally clears the 50-trade minimum, walk-forward gate passes cleanly, and the OOS/IS Sharpe relationship is healthy. The core problem is that the strategy generates genuine but weak alpha — the win rate of 42.3% and profit factor of 1.25 indicate marginally positive edge, not a strong one. The tighter stop may be causing excessive whipsaw exits rather than cutting large losers.
evaluation.next_direction: The OOS > IS Sharpe pattern and perfect walk-forward consistency confirm the signal is real — focus on improving IS Sharpe and reducing IS MaxDD without sacrificing this property. Two targeted changes: (1) Widen the trailing stop back toward 7-8% to reduce whipsaw exits that are hurting IS Sharpe and ironically worsening MaxDD by triggering re-entries at worse prices; (2) Introduce a position sizing rule — size each ETF position by inverse volatility (e.g., 20-day realized vol) so that volatile ETFs receive smaller allocations, directly capping drawdown contribution per position. Keep 5/12 SMA crossover and all 8 ETFs. Do NOT add entry filters. The goal: IS Sharpe >= 1.0 requires either higher avg win or fewer losses — volatility-scaled sizing should improve risk-adjusted returns by concentrating capital in lower-vol, more predictable trending ETFs. Target: IS MaxDD < -20% via vol-sizing, IS Sharpe > 1.0 via better risk-adjusted entries, trade count maintained above 50.
